notes: Roman emperor (r. 217-218), known for a brief and turbulent reign marked by cruelty and tyranny.

docs.title: Life of Diadumenianus
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: 3 Nevertheless, I find in many writers that he himself was cruel beyond his years, and this is shown by a letter which he sent to his father. 4 For when certain men had fallen under the suspicion of rebellion, Macrinus visited

docs.title: Life of Elagabalus
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: Dio ( LXXVIII .23.2) emphasizes his considerate treatment of Julia Domna. The statement (repeated also by Victor, Caes. xxiii.1 ) that Elagabalus fled to the temple at Emesa is a wholly incorrect inference from his permanent residence there as hereditary high-priest.

docs.title: Life of Macrinus
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … emperor, was, in spite of all, rather rigid and stern, thinking that so he could bury in oblivion all his previous career, though in fact this very sternness of his presented an opportunity for criticising and attacking him.
